title: Fix CSV/XLS import failure — all records failing to upsert to backend

requirements.text: Investigate and fix the import failure in CSVImportSection where all parsed records fail to upsert to the backend. The error toast shows 'Failed to import unit "S10026_PRD" (2026-W09) from "S10026_PRD.xls"' followed by 'All 4 records failed to import'. The unit IDs are derived from filenames (e.g. S10026_PRD, S10006-PRD, S10002-PRD). Identify why the backend upsert calls are failing — likely causes include: (1) the unit ID or week key format not matching what the backend actor expects, (2) a type mismatch in the data being sent, or (3) an error thrown by the backend actor during the create/update call. Add detailed per-record error logging to the console and ensure the error toast message includes the specific backend error reason so users can diagnose the problem.

quotes: unable to import to dash board receiving error

acceptanceCriteria: Importing valid XLS files with unit IDs like S10026_PRD, S10006-PRD, S10002-PRD for week 2026-W09 successfully upserts records to the backend without error
The 'All N records failed to import' toast no longer appears for valid files
If a record does fail, the error toast includes the specific backend error message (not just the unit ID and filename)
Successfully imported records appear on the Dashboard page under the correct week

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Unit ID must continue to be derived exclusively from the filename stem, not from file contents
nonGoals: Changing the file parsing logic for CSV/XLS/XLSX unless it is directly causing the upsert failure
Changes to the Report page or WeeklyReportTable filtering
